posts.title: Pet grief is clinically real
posts.caption: Losing a pet isn't 'just' losing a pet. Research consistently shows that the grief is neurologically and emotionally equivalent to losing a human companion. That is not sentiment. That is science. #PetLoss #PetGrief #AnimalBond

posts.title: You are not overreacting
posts.caption: Whatever you are feeling right now â€” it is real, it is valid, and it is not too much. Grief for a pet is grief. Full stop. #PetLoss #PetGrief #YouAreNotAlone

posts.title: What is TRACE?
posts.caption: TRACE is a structured method for supporting pet bereavement â€” built specifically for practitioners who want to do this well, not just with good intentions. Five letters. A complete framework. #TRACE #PetBereavement #PractitionerTraining

posts.title: A place that holds their story
posts.caption: Trace Memorial is a dedicated space for your pet's story â€” the photos, the memories, the specific things that made them who they were. Not a database. A memorial. #TraceMemorial #PetMemorial #PetLoss

posts.title: For the vet who doesn't know what to say
posts.caption: You were trained for the medicine. Nobody trained you for the silence after. The look on a client's face when nothing more can be done. There are tools for that moment. #VetLife #VeterinaryWellbeing #PetBereavement

posts.title: Stat: 1 in 3
posts.caption: Research keeps arriving at the same conclusion: pet grief is not a minor loss. It is a major one, without the social scaffolding that major losses usually receive. #PetLoss #PetGrief #AcademyForPetLoss
posts.overlay_text: 1 in 3 people say losing a pet was harder than losing a person.

posts.title: Not every loss
posts.caption: If you are carrying a loss that the world did not stop for â€” it still counts. You still count. #PetLoss #PetGrief #TraceMemorial
posts.overlay_text: Not every loss makes the front page. But every loss changes a life.

posts.title: The profession most people have never heard of
posts.caption: Pet bereavement counselling is a recognised professional field. It has a body of method, a growing evidence base, and practitioners working across clinical and non-clinical settings. Most people have never heard of it. Yet. #PetBereavement #Counselling

posts.title: People don't always understand
posts.caption: They mean well. But 'at least it was just a pet' is one of the most isolating sentences a grieving person can hear. You know what you lost. You don't need to justify it. #PetLoss #DisenfranchisedGrief #PetGrief

posts.title: The difference between sympathy and support
posts.caption: Sympathy says 'I'm sorry for your loss.' Support asks 'what do you need right now?' One is a response. The other is a skill. #PetBereavement #GriefSupport #PractitionerSkills

posts.title: Some people find their purpose here
posts.caption: For some, grief becomes a turning point. A loss so significant it raises a question: how do I help others through this? If that's you, support and training exist. #PetLoss #PetBereavement #AcademyForPetLoss

posts.title: For the person who lost their pet and found their calling
posts.caption: Some of the best practitioners in this field came to it through their own loss. A grief that changed them, followed by a question: how do I help others through this? #PetLoss #PetBereavement #FindingYourCalling

posts.title: Why vets are not trained for this moment
posts.caption: Veterinary training covers medicine, surgery, pharmacology. It rarely covers the moment a client's world falls apart in your consultation room. That gap has consequences. #VetLife #VeterinaryMedicine #PetBereavement

posts.title: The routines that remind you
posts.caption: The walk you still reach for the lead for. The food bowl you haven't moved yet. The spot on the sofa. Grief lives in the small specific things. #PetLoss #PetGrief #Remembering

posts.title: T is for...
posts.caption: Every element of the TRACE method exists for a reason. The first letter sets the foundation for everything that follows. #TRACE #PetBereavementCounselling #GriefSupport

posts.title: Their name deserves to be said
posts.caption: A Trace Memorial page keeps their name in a permanent place â€” one you can return to, share, and add to over time. #TraceMemorial #PetMemorial #PetLoss

posts.title: For the therapist whose clients keep mentioning their pets
posts.caption: It keeps coming up â€” the dog, the cat, the horse. You're not sure how much weight to give it. Your client is not sure it's allowed to matter this much. It is. #TherapistLife #PetLoss #GriefCounselling

posts.title: Five minutes
posts.caption: The answer to this question changes everything about what happens next. If you are working with clients who are grieving, it is worth sitting with. #PetBereavement #PractitionerSkills #VetLife
posts.overlay_text: What does your client need in the first five minutes after a loss?

posts.title: You are allowed
posts.caption: Grief does not expire. Remembering them does not mean you are stuck. It means you loved them. #PetGrief #PetLoss #YouAreNotAlone
posts.overlay_text: You are allowed to still miss them.

posts.title: What happens when grief has nowhere to go
posts.caption: Disenfranchised grief is grief that society doesn't validate. Pet loss is one of the clearest examples. When grief has no legitimate space, it doesn't disappear â€” it finds somewhere else to go. #DisenfranchisedGrief #PetLoss #GriefSupport

posts.title: The house is too quiet
posts.caption: The sound of them arriving home with you. The weight of them on the bed. The particular way they moved through a room. Absence has a texture. #PetGrief #PetLoss #TraceMemorial

posts.title: What good pet bereavement support actually looks like
posts.caption: It doesn't look like a script. It looks like a practitioner who is present, informed, and unhurried â€” a client who leaves the room feeling held rather than managed. #PetBereavement #CounsellingSkills

posts.title: You are not alone in this
posts.caption: Your grief is real. Support exists â€” whether that is a space to hold their memory, a community that understands, or professionals trained to walk alongside you. #PetLoss #PetGrief #YouAreNotAlone

posts.title: For the vet nurse who carries it home
posts.caption: Vet nurses hold an enormous amount. They're often the ones in the room at the end, the ones who remember the animal's name, the ones who absorb the grief the client cannot contain. This is for them. #VetNurse #VeterinaryTeam #PetBereavement

posts.title: Your clients are grieving. Are you equipped?
posts.caption: If you work with people, you work with pet owners. Many are grieving quietly â€” at the consultation, in the waiting room, during the session. The question is whether you have tools for that moment. #VetLife #TherapistLife #PetLoss

posts.title: Grief does not have a timeline
posts.caption: There is no point at which you are supposed to be over it. Grief moves at its own pace. You are allowed to still feel this. #PetGrief #GriefJourney #PetLoss

posts.title: Why most grief support gets this wrong
posts.caption: General grief models were not built with pet loss in mind. Applying them without adaptation produces support that technically exists but doesn't quite fit. TRACE was built to fit. #TRACE #PetLoss #GriefModels

posts.title: The photos you keep scrolling back to
posts.caption: The ones that captured them exactly as they were. Your Trace Memorial page is a home for those images â€” not buried in a camera roll, but held. #TraceMemorial #PetPhotos #PetMemorial

posts.title: For the animal rescue worker
posts.caption: Rescue work means loving animals and losing them regularly â€” carrying grief without ceremony because the next animal is already here. This work deserves a proper framework. #AnimalRescue #PetBereavement #RescueLife

posts.title: Profession statement
posts.caption: This work is recognised, taught, and in demand. If you have been thinking about it, the door is open. #PetBereavement #CounsellorTraining #AcademyForPetLoss
posts.overlay_text: Pet bereavement counselling. A profession. A method. A growing field.

posts.title: What would you want people to know
posts.caption: Tell us in the comments. We would love to hear their name, and a little of what made them who they were. #PetMemorial #TraceMemorial #PetLoss
posts.overlay_text: What would you want people to know about them?

posts.title: The moment that changes everything
posts.caption: Most practitioners who come to this work can name the exact moment â€” a client they couldn't help, a conversation that ended too soon, a loss they didn't know how to hold. That moment is often the beginning. #PetBereavement #PractitionerTraining

posts.title: It's okay to talk about them
posts.caption: Say their name. Tell the story again. Describe what they were like at their most themselves. Talking about them is not dwelling â€” it is loving. #PetMemorial #PetLoss #TraceMemorial

posts.title: Why pet grief does not follow the five stages
posts.caption: The KÃ¼bler-Ross model is widely known and widely misapplied. Pet bereavement grief rarely follows a linear sequence. A method built for this kind of grief needs to account for that. #PetLoss #GriefModels #TRACE

posts.title: Shared with the people who understood
posts.caption: The ones who met them, loved them, or simply understood why they mattered. A Trace Memorial page can be shared with everyone who was part of their life. #TraceMemorial #PetCommunity #PetLoss

posts.title: You don't need to be a therapist to do this
posts.caption: Pet bereavement support is not exclusively clinical work. The Academy's training is accessible to practitioners from a range of backgrounds. What you need is care and a willingness to learn. #PetBereavement #Training #CounsellorTraining

posts.title: No timeline for love
posts.caption: Grief and love move together. Neither of them keeps to a schedule. #PetGrief #PetLoss #TraceMemorial
posts.overlay_text: Grief does not have a timeline. Neither does love.

posts.title: The question that opens everything
posts.caption: There is one question â€” asked at the right moment, in the right way â€” that can shift an entire session. It is part of the TRACE method. And once you know it, you will use it every time. #TRACE #PetBereavement #CounsellingTechnique

posts.title: Some bonds are not explained, they are just felt
posts.caption: You don't need to justify what they meant to you. The bond between a person and their animal is one of the most honest relationships a human being can have. #HumanAnimalBond #PetLoss #PetGrief

posts.title: What our graduates say
posts.caption: What changes after the training isn't always what people expect. Less about knowing more, and more about being able to be present differently. #AcademyForPetLoss #PetBereavement

posts.title: Something that does not disappear
posts.caption: A Trace Memorial page is permanent. When everything else moves on, their memory has a place that stays. #TraceMemorial #PetMemorial #PetLoss

posts.title: Grief is real
posts.caption: The grief is real. The training exists. The profession is growing. #PetBereavement #AcademyForPetLoss #CounsellorTraining
posts.overlay_text: The grief is real. The training exists. The profession is growing.

posts.title: Shape of who they were
posts.caption: The space they left behind is not emptiness. It is the shape of who they were. #PetLoss #PetGrief #TraceMemorial
posts.overlay_text: The space they left is the shape of who they were.

posts.title: What grief looks like when no one is watching
posts.caption: It is not always tears. Sometimes it is sitting in a room that still smells like them. Sometimes it is talking to them anyway. Your private grief is real grief. #PetLoss #PetGrief #Grief

posts.title: Their name
posts.caption: A name said out loud. A memory held somewhere permanent. That is what a Trace Memorial page is for. #TraceMemorial #PetMemorial #PetLoss
posts.overlay_text: Their name deserves to be said out loud.
